title: Should XPeng's April Delivery Uptick and Governance Changes Require Action?
text: XPeng reported April 2026 deliveries of 31,011 vehicles, marking a 13% increase from March 2026, providing some relief after softer Q1 guidance. The monthly delivery improvement suggests potential stabilization in demand after the weakness that caused guidance cuts earlier in the year. Governance changes accompanying the delivery report indicate management is actively addressing investor concerns about strategic direction and capital allocation. The sequential improvement in deliveries could signal that the Q1 2026 guidance disappointment may represent a temporary trough rather than a sustained deceleration. However, investors remain cautious about whether XPeng can return to the growth trajectory of 2025 amid intense competitive pressures and price wars in the Chinese EV market.
